transcript: Do you want to know why I stopped you?
Do you remember what I asked you?

Well, I asked you for your driver's license and you handed me a vape pen and then you dumped out your purse and then you looked at your driver's license and didn't hand it to me, so that's where I'm at.
Do you know why I stopped you?

You made a right on red at Hilliard Madison, floored it, went through the red light at McKinley, then you turned on the Riverside, then you use a turn signal, then you started to make a left turn and then turn your signal on kind of like as you were making your left turn, made a wide right turn around the curve, and I smell alcohol. You can't tell me where you're coming from. You handed me a vape pen instead of a driver's license. So I'm going to have you step out, we're going to do some field sobriety tests, okay?
No, thank you. Okay, well then step on out.
Because you're under arrest for DUI. Okay?
Do you want to get out or do you want me to help you out?
No, thank you. It's not really an optional thing right now, ma'am.
From the jump, Miss Cloud Dummy turned a license check into a stand-up routine. Asked for ID, she proudly served up a vape pen like it was her passport to freedom.
